title: salix retusa subsp. kitaibeliana
intro: Salix retusa subsp. kitaibeliana, also known as the Hungarian willow, is a subspecies of the Salix retusa species, which is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Salix retusa subsp. kitaibeliana include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Salix retusa subsp. kitaibeliana is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
